back_translation: At that time, the Feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Feast of Passover, was drawing near.
The chief priests and the scribes were seeking a way to do away with Him, because they feared the people.
Now Satan entered into Judas, who is called Iscariot, who was one of the twelve.
Then Judas went and consulted with the chief priests and the officers of the temple-guard about the way he would hand Him over to them.
They were all glad and agreed to give him money.
Judas consented, and was looking for an opportunity to hand Him over to them at a time when there was no crowd.
The day of Unleavened Bread had come, the day on which the Passover lamb must be slaughtered.
Jesus sent Peter and John, saying, 'Go and prepare the Passover for us so that we may eat.'
They asked Him, 'Where do You wish us to go and prepare it?'
He said to them, 'Behold, when you enter the city, a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him to the house he enters,
and say to the owner of the house: The Teacher asks you, Where is the guest-room where I will eat the Passover with My disciples?'
And the owner of the house will show you a large upper room already prepared. Prepare the Passover there.
So they went and found things exactly as He had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
When the time came, He reclined down to eat, along with the apostles.
He said to them, 'I have greatly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
I tell you that I will not eat the Passover again until the Passover is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.'
He took the cup; when He had given thanks, He said, 'Take this cup and divide it to drink among yourselves.
I tell you, from now on I will not drink the water from the fruit of the vine again until the kingdom of God comes.'
Then He took bread; when He had given thanks, He broke it and gave it to them, saying, 'This is My body which is given up for you. Do this to remember Me.'
In the same way, after the evening meal, He took the cup, saying, 'This cup is the new covenant in My blood, which is poured out for you.
But behold, the hand of the one who will hand Me over is here together with Me at this table already.
For the Son of Man will go as it has been determined, but woe to that man through whose action the Son of Man will be handed over.'
They began to question one another: who among them would do this thing?
Then a dispute arose among them as to which of them should be considered the greatest.
He said to them, 'The kings of the Gentiles are masters over the people, and those who have authority over them are called "benefactors."
But you are not like that. On the contrary, the greatest among you, let him be like the least, and the one who leads like the one who serves.
For who is greater: the one who reclines to eat, or the one who serves? Is it not the one who reclines to eat? But I am among you as one who serves.
But you are those who have stood fast with Me in the times when I was tested.
Therefore I make a covenant granting a kingdom to you, just as My Father has made a covenant granting a kingdom to Me,
so that you may eat and drink at My table in My kingdom, and you will sit upon thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.'
'Simon, O Simon, behold, Satan has asked permission to take you all to sift like one sifts wheat.
But I have prayed for you already, that your faith will not fail. And when you have turned back, strengthen your brothers.'
Peter said to Him, 'O Lord, I am ready to go with You both into prison and to death.'
He said, 'I tell you, O Peter, the rooster will not crow today until you deny three times that you know Me.'
He said to them, 'When I sent you out without money-bag, without knapsack, without sandals, did you lack anything?' They answered, 'Nothing at all, Lord.'
He said to them, 'But now, the one who has a money-bag, let him take it; likewise a knapsack; and the one who does not have a sword, let him sell his cloak to buy a sword.

I tell you that this scripture must be fulfilled in Me, that which says, "And he was numbered with the transgressors of the law." For what is spoken concerning Me is about to be fulfilled completely.'
They said, 'O Lord, behold, here are two swords.' He said to them, 'That is enough.'
He went out to the Mount of Olives as was His custom, and the disciples followed Him.
When He came to that place, He said to them, 'Pray that you may not enter into temptation.'
He withdrew from them about a stone's throw distance, and knelt down to pray,
praying, 'O Father, if You are pleased, please remove this cup from Me. But not as I will, let it be as You will.'
[[Then an angel from heaven appeared to Him to strengthen Him.]]
[[When He was in deep agony, He prayed even more earnestly, and His sweat was like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.]]
When He rose from prayer and came to the disciples, He found them sleeping because of sorrow.
He said to them, 'Why are you sleeping? Get up and pray so that you will not enter into temptation.'
While He was still speaking, behold, a crowd came, and Judas, one of the twelve, was leading them. He approached Jesus to kiss Him.
But Jesus said to him, 'O Judas, are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?'
When the disciples around Him saw what was about to happen, they said, 'O Lord, should we strike with the sword?'
One of them struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
Jesus answered, 'Stop, do not do that anymore!' Then He touched the ear of that man and healed him.
Jesus said to the chief priests, the officers of the temple guard, and the elders who came out to arrest Him, 'Are you carrying swords and clubs to arrest Me as you would arrest a bandit?
When I was with you in the temple courtyard every day, you did not stretch out your hands to seize Me. But this hour is yours and belongs to the authority of darkness.'
Then they seized Him, led Him away, and brought Him into the house of the high priest. But Peter followed at a distance.
When people kindled a fire in the middle of the courtyard and sat down together, Peter also sat down among them.
A servant-girl saw Peter sitting near the firelight, and when she stared at him, she said, 'This man was also with Him.'
But Peter denied it and said, 'Woman, I do not know Him.'
A little while later, another person saw him and said, 'You are also one of them.' But Peter replied, 'Man, I am not.'
About an hour later, another person insisted firmly, 'Truly this man was with Him also, for he is a Galilean.'
Peter answered, 'Man, I do not know what you are speaking about.' At that very moment, while Peter was still speaking, the rooster crowed.
The Lord turned and looked at Peter, and Peter remembered the word that the Lord had spoken to him: 'Before the rooster crows today, you will deny Me three times.'
Peter went outside and wept bitterly.
The guards who were holding Him began to mock and beat Him.
They put a cloth over His eyes, then asked Him, 'Prophesy! Who is the one who struck You?'
And they spoke many other insulting words against Him.
When morning came, the council of the elders of the people, composed of the chief priests and the scribes, assembled together. They led Him to their Sanhedrin council.
They said, 'If You are the Christ, tell us.' He said to them, 'If I tell you, you will not believe.
And if I ask you, you will not answer.
But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right hand of God who is all-powerful.'
They all said, 'Are You then the Son of God?' He said to them, 'You yourselves say that I am.'
They said, 'What further witnesses do we need? For we ourselves have heard from his own mouth.'
